channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Antitrust cases against Big Tech and megamergers could end in deals under Trump, experts say
item.description: The U.S. antitrust watchdogs that pounced on Big Tech and deterred corporate deal-making throughout President Joe Biden’s administration may be kept on a shorter leash by Donald Trump after he returns to the White House next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Yellowjackets Sued for Copyright Infringement Over Plot Similarities to Survival Thriller Eden
item.description: Showtime's Yellowjackets is being sued for copyright infringement over plot similarities to the 2015 survival thriller Eden.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Trump’s pick for FCC chairman is Brendan Carr
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Sunday named Brendan Carr, the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission, as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications, and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational
item.description: The ETAC has quickly scaled from a pilot project to an operational center at the Energy Department for analyzing cyber threats like Volt Typhoon. The post Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia Blackwell chips face serious heating issues
item.description: Nvidia’s next-generation Blackwell data center processors have significant problems with overheating when installed in high-capacity server racks, forcing redesigns of the racks themselves, according to a report by The Information.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Roblox debuts new child protection, parental control features
item.description: Following recent questions about child safety issues on the platform, Roblox announced Monday a number of changes designed to protect young users and increase parental control over the content they see.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Beyond the checkbox: Building confidence in your DR strategy
item.description: Disaster recovery (DR) is often no more than a checkbox item for IT, because the team spends so much time fighting fires and working to advance new, mission-critical implementations. To that point, DR didn’t make CIOs’ top five priorities in the Foundry State of the CIO 2024 survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How does Elon Musk define ‘efficiency’? We asked his former employees
item.description: Elon Musk has big plans for his new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) once Donald Trump takes the White House in January. Prior to the election, the Tesla CEO said he would help cut $2 trillion from the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Vaio releases 999g, 14-inch laptop with up to 64GB of RAM, 5G SIM — and even a physical Gigabit Ethernet port
item.description: 999g, 14-inch Vaio SX14-R laptop offers up to 64GB of RAM, 5G SIM and a physical Gigabit Ethernet port.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: All hail our new leader — AMD-powered El Capitan becomes officially the world’s fastest supercomputer
item.description: El Capitan delivers 1.7 exaflops of sustained performance for Lawrence Livermore national laboratory.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: About 1 in 5 Americans say they get their news from social media influencers, according to a new poll from the Pew Research Center. Of the 21 percent of adults who said they get their news from influencers on social media, the split was roughly even across parties.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Unlocking Business Insights with Unit Economics: A Cloud FinOps Perspective
item.description: Exploring the role of Unit Economics in driving business value and cost optimization through cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: A report released Monday by Pew Research Center says about one in five Americans regularly get their news from so-called news influencers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fermyon Releases Spin 3.0: A Major Update to Its Open Source Developer Tool
item.description: Fermyon's latest release brings polyglot programming, deeper integration with WASI standards, and support for OpenTelemetry observability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The dirty little secret of open source contributions
item.description: “Nobody cares if you contribute.” That’s what a Postgres friend said to me during lunch at KubeCon when I suggested that hiring Postgres contributors could be a selling point for customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sólo el 21% de las empresas se apoya en la tecnología para diseñar estrategias de sostenibilidad
item.description: A pesar de que el 84% de las organizaciones considera de importancia “estratégica” sus objetivos de sostenibilidad, sólo el 21% de ellas se apoya en la tecnología para reducir su huella medioambiental y configurar su estrategia global en este ámbito.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FTC Prepares Investigation into Microsoft's Cloud Practices
item.description: US Federal Trade Commission prepares investigation into Microsoft for potentially anticompetitive practices in cloud computing division.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says industry was "intensely engaged" in making the new DHS guidelines, and it's up to companies to adopt them. The post DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk: DOGE will be 'tedious work' with 'zero' compensation
item.description: Elon Musk said in a post on the social platform X on Thursday that the brand new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — an advisory group under President-elect Trump focused on slashing government costs and restructuring federal agencies — will require "tedious work" with "zero" compensation.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: AI is everywhere. How should California schools handle it? Teachers’ different approaches show its potential — and limits
item.description: 'We’re trying to teach the ethical use of these new tools that are just going to keep on growing,' one teacher says.
item.dc:creator: Kristen Taketa, The Mercury News

item.title: Only 8 monkeys remain free after more than a week outside a South Carolina compound
item.description: Authorities say Just eight monkeys remain free from the group who broke out of a South Carolina compound that breeds the primates for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cloud creates network blind spots, complicates problem resolution
item.description: As network environments become more complex, network teams struggle to gain visibility across internet and cloud components, citing blind spots and a lack of skilled workers as contributing factors to slow problem resolution, according to a Broadcom report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CISA’s SILENTSHIELD assessment requires urgent measures
item.description: The SILENTSHIELD red team assessment has highlighted the urgent need for enhanced cyber resilience within federal agencies. The post CISA’s SILENTSHIELD assessment requires urgent measures first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation
